role_summary: Implements user-interface and feedback functions that are not placed directly on the main board.
Bridges the main board to local buttons, LEDs, and the haptic motor driver through a 5-pin connector.
Uses a local I2C GPIO expander so the main board only needs I2C plus one interrupt line.

design_relevance: Generates GPIO interrupt back to host
Generates LRA_EN control for haptic driver
Drives RGB LED and multiple red LEDs
Needs only shared I2C access from host side
Enable input is sourced from GPIO expander, not directly from main MCU

firmware_implications: The physical trigger/buttons may not land directly on ESP32 GPIOs; they are likely observable through the MCP23017 over I2C.
User indicators and haptic feedback are auxiliary-board-managed peripherals on the same I2C segment.
GPIO_INT_A is the likely event line the main MCU can use to avoid pure polling of the expander.
open_points: Cross-board mapping is still needed to prove which main-board connector/pins carry I2C_SCL, I2C_SDA, and GPIO_INT_A.
The exact intended semantic role of each button still depends on system-level UX definition, even though the electrical mapping is now clear.
